reviews.review_description:
It was a nice experience to practive with combat master.
The graphic quality and physical response were enough for immersed into punching practice and others.
Only one hope is Local Language support. Even just English would be appreciated.
Merci Lionel!

FYYTE is a combative fitness game: not just empty punches, but real rounds, opponents, and some serious cardio.
If you like to move and sweat, it delivers.
FYYTE has a lot of potential: the boxing sensations are there, the rounds provide a real cardio workout, and the idea of ​​training with a champion adds a serious touch.
The multiplayer mode also allows you to challenge other players, which is a change from many single-player fitness apps.
Visually, it's beautiful: the arenas are clear, the punch feedback is satisfying, and the controller controls are precise.
However, the game is still in development, and it shows.
Some opponents or levels lack variety, and sessions can become repetitive.
Comfort is sometimes challenged (impact of blows, muscle fatigue), and proper breaks are essential!
The lack of MR or the ability to use gestures without a controller might disappoint those expecting more immersion.
For those who enjoy hardcore fitness, boxing, and competition, this is an excellent boxing game in the making!
Looking forward to the upcoming updates, because the training mode alone is already a lot of fun!

FYYTE is a must-try for nostalgic fans of the legendary kickboxer Jérôme Le Banner — the French powerhouse with a wall of fame as a 2-time K-1 World Grand Prix runner-up, 2-time K-1 Preliminary Grand Prix champion, and multiple-time Kickboxing and Muay Thai world champion.

It’s a true treat to spar with Le Banner on pads: you immediately feel the presence, power, and mass of his avatar. When he charges in and you have to dodge, the intensity feels real. The scoring system is sharp, and the game rewards fast, well-timed combinations that push you to keep improving.

Currently in early access, some features like career mode and multiplayer are still greyed out, but the core gameplay already delivers. With those modes likely coming soon, it’s shaping up to be a complete combat sports experience.

For now, the game is only available in English (voices and subtitles), which also makes it a fun way to “muscle up” your French skills while training with a legend.
I downloaded it and it was a different language and it’s so bad don’t download it
